section_miscategorizations.reason: This would normally be appropriate in Plan, but in this note the hemoglobin A1c result (8%) is already documented in O, implying the test has already been performed. As written, it reads as a new order for a test that has already occurred, creating organizational confusion between past objective data and future orders.

section_miscategorizations.text: Continue metformin 500 mg twice a day.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a treatment/medication management action and belongs in the Plan. However, because there is no prior mention of metformin in S or O, it is unclear whether this is a true continuation versus a new prescription, which slightly blurs the boundary between documenting current meds (S) and management changes (P).
